key_topics: Number: reading and ordering numbers to 1000
Rounding to the nearest 10 or 100
Addition and subtraction of three-digit numbers
Multiplication tables: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10
Multiplication of two-digit by one-digit numbers
Division: dividing two-digit numbers by one-digit numbers
Fractions: finding fractions of amounts, equivalent fractions
Comparing and ordering fractions with the same denominator
Decimals: tenths as decimals and fractions
Geometry: properties of 2D shapes — sides, angles
Right angles: identifying and comparing
Perimeter of rectilinear shapes
Area: counting squares
Measurement: metric units for length, mass, capacity
Time: reading time to the minute, 24-hour clock
Data: tables, bar charts, pictograms, Carroll and Venn diagrams
competences: Add and subtract three-digit numbers with regrouping
Find fractions of amounts and identify equivalent fractions
Read and interpret data from bar charts and pictograms
Use metric units confidently for measurement
typical_mistakes: Errors in multiplication tables for 6 and 9
Thinking equivalent fractions are different values

key_topics: Reading longer texts: chapters, non-fiction books
Comprehension: inference, deduction, authorial intent
Story structure: beginning, build-up, climax, resolution
Descriptive writing: using similes, adjectives, adverbs
Informational writing: non-chronological reports
Instructional writing: sequential steps, imperative verbs
Persuasive writing: reasons, emotive language
Grammar: conjunctions for time, cause, contrast
Apostrophes: possession and contraction
Paragraphs: organizing writing into paragraphs
Vocabulary: word families, prefixes (un-, dis-, mis-, re-)
competences: Read and respond to a variety of text types with inference
Write stories with clear structure and descriptive language
Use paragraphs to organize non-fiction writing
Apply apostrophes for possession and contraction correctly
typical_mistakes: Apostrophe errors (it's vs. its, your vs. you're)
Similes that are not effective or mixed up with metaphors
Over-reliance on common adjectives (nice, big, good)

key_topics: Life processes: movement, reproduction, sensitivity, growth, respiration, excretion, nutrition (MRS GREN)
Habitats: how plants and animals adapt to their environment
Food webs: multiple interconnected food chains
States of matter: solids, liquids, gases — particle model
Changing materials: reversible and irreversible changes
Forces: gravity, friction, magnetic forces
Magnets: poles, attraction and repulsion
Light: reflection, shadows and their changes
competences: Identify the seven life processes using MRS GREN
Construct food webs from food chains
Investigate magnetic forces and describe results
typical_mistakes: Thinking MRS GREN applies only to animals, not plants
Confusing reversible and irreversible changes (melting is reversible)
